title: smoky tender energizing tunisian leek tagine
description: a hearty leek-based dish from tunisia that's both nourishing and flavorful. served with a side of steaming couscous and garnished with fresh parsley.

ingredients.name: large leeks
ingredients.notes: chopped and cleaned of roots and outer layers

ingredients.name: ground cumin
ingredients.notes: ground freshly in mortar and pestle

ingredients.name: ground coriander
ingredients.notes: ground freshly in mortar and pestle

ingredients.name: olive oil
ingredients.notes: use a high-smoke-point oil like avocado or grapeseed

instructions: heat the comal over medium heat and toast the cumin and coriander seeds until fragrant and slightly darkened
grind the toasted spices into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle
heat the olive oil in a large clay pot or Dutch oven over medium heat and saut  the leeks until they're softened and lightly browned
add the onion and garlic to the pot and cook until the onion is translucent
add the ground spices and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly
add the chickpeas, chicken broth, and cayenne pepper (if using) to the pot
bring the mixture to a simmer and let it cook for 20-25 minutes or until the liquid has reduced by half and the leeks are tender
season the tagine with salt and pepper to taste
serve the smoky tender energizing tunisian leek tagine hot over steaming couscous
garnish with fresh parsley and a dollop of yogurt (optional)
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
